concerns.text: FR-001 (Data Retrieval & Exclusion Logging) lacks a dedicated task. While T010 handles fetching and T011 handles preprocessing, no task explicitly implements the logic to 'log the exclusion' of samples missing modalities as mandated by FR-001. The plan requires this logging for provenance (Constitution Principle VI), but the tasks only mention 'wrappers' and 'alignment' without the specific logging requirement.

FR-002 (Preprocessing) is partially covered by T011, but the task description 'wrappers for fastp... and MetaboAnalyst-compatible normalization' does not explicitly confirm the generation of the 'aligned feature tables ready for joint analysis' required by FR-002. A specific task or sub-task ensuring the alignment logic (matching sample IDs across modalities) is distinct from the tool wrappers is missing.

FR-004 (Model Training) requires reporting performance against a 'null model baseline (random labels)'. T018 covers permutation testing (FR-005) and T017 covers model training, but no task explicitly implements the generation/training of the null model baseline or the comparison logic required by FR-004. T022 generates metrics but does not specify the inclusion of the null model comparison.

FR-006 (Docker & CI Constraints) requires the pipeline to complete within 6h/7GB on GitHub Actions. While T006 sets up Docker and T038 optimizes performance, there is no specific task to 'document all commands' as required by FR-006, nor a task to explicitly verify the CI constraints (runtime/memory) during the build process (T040 validates quickstart, not the specific resource constraints of FR-006).

SC-002 (Success Criteria: 10 SNPs/10 Metabolites) requires that 'At least 10 SNPs and 10 metabolites remain significant... across the entire sensitivity sweep'. T028 filters features, but no task explicitly implements the logic to count and verify this specific threshold (10/10) across the sweep results, nor to report this specific success criterion in the final artifacts.

FR-009 (Data Splitting) requires splitting into training and hold-out sets with specific proportions (deferred). T015 and T031 implement the split logic, but no task explicitly addresses the 'strict reservation' of the hold-out set to ensure it is never used in feature selection or training, other than the code implementation itself. A specific validation task or logic check for this strict separation is missing.
